refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and the operational context (agreed network conditions), ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and compare against customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

candidate_dps: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
Use dedicated high-reliability network link with QoS prioritization.
Deploy real-time packet loss monitoring and alerting system.
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Dependency on network link quality and telemetry data.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring to balance packet loss and responsiveness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control, communication management, and related telemetry processing.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Incoming telemetry data from vehicle sensors relevant to communication status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and control signals sent to the vehicle.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Vehicle module primarily hardware-focused; poor containment of communication protocol logic.
High containment score due to direct responsibility for teleoperation communication.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety policies, not communication protocol.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, reducing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Safety Services if communication quality impacts safety alerts.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate interface definitions with Safety Services to manage cross-cutting concerns.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with a clear mechanism to control packet loss.

diffusion_risks: Minor risk of communication logic spreading into NGHE Solution or Safety Services modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows strong containment and low diffusion scores, supporting the assignment.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics within the Teleoperation module during system tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of communication quality management within Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Add latency monitoring to DP to balance packet loss and responsiveness.
Clarify interface contracts between Teleoperation and Safety Services for safety-critical communication events.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and verification criteria. No immediate need to zigzag or revise current level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to relevant modules.

revise.why: In future levels, incorporate latency monitoring and adaptive balancing with packet loss control.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module has capability to implement adaptive communication protocols.
open_risks: Latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness due to retransmissions.
Interface coordination with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
evidence_needed: Test data on packet loss and latency under representative network conditions.
Interface specification documents between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
